sn45.1:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn45.1:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
sn45.1:2.1: “Monks, ignorance is the leader in the attainment of unskillful qualities, followed by lack of shame & lack of compunction.
sn45.1:2.2: In an unknowledgeable person, immersed in ignorance, wrong view arises.
sn45.1:2.3: In one of wrong view, wrong resolve arises.
sn45.1:2.4: In one of wrong resolve, wrong speech.…
sn45.1:2.5: In one of wrong speech, wrong action.…
sn45.1:2.6: In one of wrong action, wrong livelihood.…
sn45.1:2.7: In one of wrong livelihood, wrong effort.…
sn45.1:2.8: In one of wrong effort, wrong mindfulness.…
sn45.1:2.9: In one of wrong mindfulness, wrong concentration arises.
sn45.1:3.1: “Clear knowing is the leader in the attainment of skillful qualities, followed by shame & compunction.
sn45.1:3.2: In a knowledgeable person, immersed in clear knowing, right view arises.
sn45.1:3.3: In one of right view, right resolve arises.
sn45.1:3.4: In one of right resolve, right speech.…
sn45.1:3.5: In one of right speech, right action.…
sn45.1:3.6: In one of right action, right livelihood.…
sn45.1:3.7: In one of right livelihood, right effort.…
sn45.1:3.8: In one of right effort, right mindfulness.…
sn45.1:3.9: In one of right mindfulness, right concentration arises.”
